title: lepidagathis macrantha
intro: Lepidagathis macrantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the "wild jasmine" due to its fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lepidagathis macrantha include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antiviral and anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, or used in traditional remedies such as decoctions and poultices.
